title: She’s running the Boston Marathon for her former patient with Morquio syndrome
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Erin Donnelly, a physical therapist from Boston, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of her former patient Gio.
Gio Thompson was a 5-year-old with Morquio syndrome who inspired Donnelly with his determination and personality.
Donnelly's participation honors Gio's memory, reflecting the lessons and impact the young boy had on her life.

title: Bruins fall to Devils 4-3 in OT as US Olympic hero Jack Hughes has 3 assists
author: The Associated Press
bullets: Bruins lose 4-3 in overtime to Devils, jeopardizing their playoff spot while Detroit overtakes them in the standings.
Jack Hughes assisted on all three Devils' goals, becoming the fastest player in franchise history to reach 400 career points.
David Pastrnak scored twice for Boston, but Bruins failed to secure back-to-back victories since late January.

title: Tyngsborough man charged with murder after human remains found buried under garage floor, police say
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Shawn Sullivan, 40, charged with murder after human remains found under Tyngsborough garage, suspected to be missing Lowell woman.
Police believe remains are Jill T. Kloppenburg, missing since January 2025; friends reported Sullivan's alleged confession.
Investigators discovered patched area in Sullivan's garage using radar, uncovering a bag with human remains inside.

title: Why a Texas mom is running the Boston Marathon for her son — and his MGH doctor
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Adrienne Felterman, 45, from Houston, Texas, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Massachusetts General Hospital.
Felterman is raising funds in honor of her son Charles, who has Dup15q syndrome, and his MGH doctor, Ronald Thibert.
Charles has been treated by Dr. Thibert for epilepsy at MGH for 14 years, significantly improving his quality of life.

title: ‘She is truly a superhero’: Why a Belmont athlete is running Boston for his wife
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Ben Flowers, 35, from Belmont, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of his wife, Coney Flowers.
Coney Flowers was diagnosed with Acute Lymphoblastic leukemia in 2024 and is now nearly two years cancer-free.
Ben raises funds for Caring for a Cure, which supported them through Coney's treatment and provides various patient services.

title: She’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support kids with cancer
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Kathleen Labrie, 33, from Southampton, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Mass General's Pediatric Cancer team.
Donors to Labrie's run can honor specific individuals affected by pediatric cancer with the 'I Run For Them' initiative.
Labrie's motivation includes personal connections and the stories of children like Maddie, Ashton, and Meg impacted by cancer.

title: Noah Kahan announces premiere date for documentary coming to Netflix
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan's documentary, 'Out of Body,' premieres on Netflix April 13.
The film chronicles Kahan's life, music, and journey navigating fame and mental health.
Kahan's fourth album, 'The Great Divide,' will release on April 24, following the documentary.

title: Jon Keller, longtime WBZ political analyst, returns to TV
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Jon Keller returns to WBZ as a 'special contributor' after being laid off last fall due to company restructuring.
Keller will provide political analysis and moderate major debates; his 'Keller At Large' show resumes this Sunday.
Keller, a 12-time Emmy winner, will continue his MASSterList column and contribute to Boston magazine occasionally.

title: ‘A relic of a bygone era’: Here’s why readers want Massachusetts to stop changing its clocks
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Senate President Karen E. Spilka supports a bill to end Massachusetts' biannual time change, proposing a shift to Atlantic Standard Time.
An informal poll of over 470 Boston.com readers shows 83% favor ending the time change, though opinions differ on which permanent time system to adopt.
Critics of the proposal cite concerns about darker winter mornings and coordination issues with neighboring New England states if time zone changes occur.

title: Impaired Stoneham teen drove 118 mph, fled police, and then crashed, N.H. authorities say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: An 18-year-old from Stoneham allegedly drove 118 mph and fled police before crashing in New Hampshire.
The teenager failed to stop for Maine State Police before being pursued in New Hampshire by a trooper.
He was charged with felony reckless conduct, DUI, disobeying an officer, and drug possession, and is held without bail.
